Answer: Semantic tags clearly define meaning, like <header>, <footer>, <article>.
Inline does not start a new line, block takes full width, inline-block allows block features inline.
ID is unique per page, class can be reused across elements.
Event bubbling means events propagate from child to parent elements.
AJAX allows asynchronous communication with servers without reloading pages.
localStorage stores data permanently, sessionStorage clears on close, cookies are small server-stored data.
REST API uses HTTP methods for communication like GET, POST, PUT, DELETE.
SQL is relational with fixed schema, NoSQL is flexible with unstructured data.
React hooks let you use state and lifecycle in functional components.
Server-side rendering loads content on server, client-side renders in browser.
Var is function-scoped, let is block-scoped, const is block-scoped and constant.
Flexbox arranges elements in flexible containers.
CSS Grid arranges content in rows and columns.
Responsive design adjusts layout to device screen sizes.
Relative moves relative to itself, absolute relative to parent, fixed to viewport.
DOM is Document Object Model representing web page structure.
== checks value, === checks value and type.
Closure is a function that remembers variables from its outer scope.
Promise represents a future value from asynchronous code.
Async/await simplifies handling asynchronous code.
Single-page applications dynamically update content without reloads.
Node.js is a runtime for JavaScript on the server.
Middleware in Express.js handles requests before reaching route handler.
Components are reusable UI pieces in React.
JSX is JavaScript XML, used to write HTML inside JS.
Virtual DOM is a lightweight copy of real DOM used in React.
Props are inputs to components, state is internal data.
Lifecycle methods run at different points in component’s life.
Redux manages state in applications with predictable patterns.
CORS is Cross-Origin Resource Sharing, allows resources from different domains.
PUT updates resources, POST creates new ones.
Authentication verifies identity, authorization checks permissions.
Web sockets provide two-way communication between client and server.
GraphQL is a query language for APIs with flexible data fetching.
PWAs are apps that behave like native apps in browsers.
Webpack bundles JavaScript files and assets.
Babel is a transpiler for using next-gen JavaScript features.
Hoisting means variable and function declarations are moved to top.
Arrow functions are concise functions using => syntax.
Service worker is a script that runs in background for caching and offline.
GET retrieves data, POST sends data to server.
HTTP codes indicate request result, like 200 OK, 404 Not Found.
Cookies store small data on client for session management.
JSON is a lightweight data format for communication.
MVC separates model, view, and controller in applications.
Two-way binding syncs data between UI and logic.
RESTful APIs follow principles like statelessness, resource-based URLs.
XSS is injecting malicious scripts into web applications.
SQL injection is inserting malicious SQL queries into input fields.
Use HTTPS, input validation, authentication, and secure coding practices.
